name: Openforce Independent Contractor Management API
what_it_does: Openforce REST API for independent contractor (1099) onboarding, compliance, and payments management platform. Enables AI agents to manage contractor onboarding workflow automation including background checks, insurance verification, and document collection, handle worker classification compliance documentation for IC vs. employee distinction, access contractor agreement and contract management data for legal compliance, retrieve contractor payment and 1099 tax form management data for payment processing, handle occupational accident insurance and contractor insurance verification data, access contractor performance and work history data for contractor management, manage deduction and advance management data for contractor pay management, retrieve contract renewal and status tracking data, handle contractor background screening workflow integration, and integrate contractor management data with TMS, delivery management, and gig economy workforce platforms.
use_cases: Onboarding contractors from gig platform compliance agents
Verifying contractor insurance from risk management agents
Processing contractor payments from 1099 payment agents
Tracking worker classification from compliance monitoring agents
not_for: W-2 employee management without independent contractor context
International contractor management outside US labor law framework
Simple freelance platform without multi-state IC compliance complexity
best_when: A trucking company, delivery platform, gig economy operator, or staffing firm using Openforce wants AI agents to automate contractor onboarding, IC compliance documentation, insurance verification, contractor payments, and 1099 tax management for large 1099 contractor workforces.
avoid_when: LEGAL RISK: Worker classification (IC vs. employee) is a legal determination with significant labor law consequences — automated IC classification based on Openforce data without employment attorney review creates AB5 (California), FLSA, and IRS misclassification liability. Automated contractor agreements without jurisdiction-specific legal review create contract enforceability issues. 1099 payment automation must comply with IRS backup withholding rules for TIN mismatches.

auth.notes: Openforce uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Per-client credentials. Webhooks for contractor status and payment events. REST API with JSON. Phoenix, Arizona HQ. Founded 2001 (originally TransAm Logistics Services). VC-backed. Strong in trucking, logistics, and delivery company 1099 contractor management. 450,000+ contractor records. Occupational accident (OA) insurance as key differentiator. Competes with Lancer Insurance and traditional owner-operator programs for truck driver IC management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-contractor or subscription pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-contractor pricing; platform fee plus insurance and payment services
pricing.notes: Phoenix, Arizona. Founded 2001. VC-backed. 450,000+ contractor records. Trucking, final mile delivery, and logistics primary market. Occupational accident insurance, contractor compliance documentation, and 1099 payment management. Used by TMS-integrated delivery platforms and trucking companies. Competes with traditional owner-operator leasing programs and Lancer Insurance for trucking IC management.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented per client

known_agent_gotchas: LEGAL RISK: Worker classification is a legal determination — automated IC classification documentation without employment attorney review for multi-state operations creates AB5 and FLSA misclassification liability
California AB5 complexity — California IC classification tests (ABC test) are stricter than federal FLSA; automated classification in California requires jurisdiction-specific legal validation
1099 TIN matching — IRS B-Notice for TIN/name mismatch requires backup withholding; automated payment without TIN validation creates IRS penalty exposure

Occupational accident vs. workers comp — Openforce OA insurance is not workers compensation; in states requiring workers comp, automated contractor engagement without proper coverage creates employer liability
Multi-state IC agreement compliance — IC agreements have different enforceability requirements by state; automated agreement generation must apply state-specific terms
Background check FCRA compliance — contractor background checks require FCRA adverse action process; automated contractor rejection based on background results must follow FCRA procedure
agent_readiness.security_notes: Contractor compliance and payments. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US. Independent contractor classification and 1099 payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: IC contractor management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Contractor compliance and payment data.
